lifecycle.note: Every block and variant, for design iteration.

blocks.title: Every component, one artifact
blocks.lede: A working catalog of every Dossier block and its variants, rendered from one `*.dossier.json` model. Use it to design against the real product surface, not a mockup.

blocks.title: Structure & prose
blocks.subtitle: Containers and long-form text: prose, nested sections, two-col, tabbed panes.

blocks.heading: Authoring long-form copy
blocks.markdown:
Prose blocks carry the connective narrative between heavier components. They accept inline markdown so you can emphasize a **decision**, reference a `config.flag`, or point at the [block catalog](https://example.com/blocks) without leaving the flow.

Use them to set context before a table or chart, then summarize the takeaway after. A single prose block can hold several paragraphs, which keeps related thoughts together instead of fragmenting them across cards.

They also render lists cleanly:

- **Scannable** beats dense: short lines win in a design lab.
- Inline `code` stays legible against the body text color.
- Links such as [the schema](https://example.com/schema) keep their accent treatment.

Close with a plain paragraph so the reader returns to the baseline rhythm before the next block.

blocks.title: Framed nested section
blocks.subtitle: A bordered container that groups a callout and a table at one level of depth.

blocks.title: Containers can nest.
blocks.body: This callout lives inside a framed `section`. The frame draws a hairline border so grouped content reads as one unit.

blocks.title: Container blocks at a glance
rows: Group related blocks under a heading
Place two stacks side by side

blocks.title: Unframed nested section
blocks.subtitle: Same container, no border: useful when you want grouping without a visible box.

blocks.markdown: An unframed section keeps the title and spacing semantics but drops the border. It is the right choice when nested content already has its own visual weight, like the stat strip below, and an extra frame would feel heavy.

left.heading: Left column
left.markdown: The two-col block places two independent stacks side by side. Each column nests its own blocks, so the left side can carry narrative while the right side carries structured detail.

left.title: Pairing tip.
left.body: Lead with prose on the left, then back it with a concrete artifact on the right.

cards.title: Balance
cards.body: Keep columns roughly equal in height for a clean grid.

cards.title: Reflow
cards.body: Columns stack vertically on narrow viewports.

blocks.heading: What tabs are for
blocks.markdown: Tabs swap between alternate views of the same idea without lengthening the page. Each tab nests a different kind of block, so a reader can move from **narrative** to **data** to **code** at their own pace.

blocks.title: At a glance
blocks.subtitle: Scannable summaries: summary cards, KPI stats, flow, timeline, callouts.

cards.title: Scope
cards.body: One package, zero runtime deps. Authored as JSON, shipped as a single self-contained `.html` file.

cards.title: For humans
cards.body: Reads like a polished brief. Skim the cards, scan the stats, follow the flow.

cards.title: For agents
cards.body: An embedded `dossier-model` island carries the source of truth for round-tripping edits.

cards.title: On track
cards.body: Catalog parity reached across the CLI and React renderers. All snapshots **green**.

cards.title: Watch
cards.body: Mermaid diagrams need Playwright at build time, otherwise they fall back to source.

cards.title: Blocking
cards.body: Legacy exports still emit the old `delta` shape. Migrate before the next release.

steps.title: Author
steps.body: Write the document as a `dossier.json` model with typed blocks.

steps.title: Validate
steps.body: Run the schema check so every block uses only documented fields.

steps.title: Generate
steps.body: Build a single self-contained HTML artifact plus the Markdown source.

steps.title: Hand off
steps.body: Share the file. An agent reads the embedded model and applies edits back.

phases.label: Foundation
phases.body: Schema, runtime, and the core block catalog landed.

phases.label: Process packets
phases.body: Interactive boards, gates, and round-trip export shipping now.

phases.label: React parity
phases.body: Bring every block to the React renderer at full fidelity.

phases.label: Public docs site
phases.body: Held until the legacy `delta` export format is retired.

blocks.title: Heads up
blocks.body: Every text field accepts inline markdown, so `code`, **bold**, and [links](https://example.com) all render.

blocks.title: Verified
blocks.body: The full snapshot suite passed on the release branch. No visual regressions detected.

blocks.title: Mind the build step
blocks.body: Mermaid diagrams only rasterize when Playwright is installed. Without it, the source is shown instead.

blocks.title: Breaking change ahead
blocks.body: The old export shape is deprecated. Consumers reading the legacy format will break after the next major.

blocks.title: Pro tip
blocks.body: Give collection items stable kebab-case ids so exported decisions and edits stay addressable across runs.

blocks.title: Reference
blocks.subtitle: Tables, code, editable code, FAQ, sources, glossary.

blocks.title: Block ownership (text columns)
rows: Accordion, one open at a time

blocks.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

npm ci
npm test
npm run build

VERSION="$(node -p "require('./package.json').version")"
echo "Publishing dossier@${VERSION}"
npm publish --access public

blocks.title: Editable runtime config
blocks.summary: Bounded source text that can be edited in place and exported as an edits packet.

items.q: Do tables support markdown inside cells?
items.a: Yes. Cell text accepts inline markdown such as **bold**, `code`, and [links](https://example.com).
Shiki runs at build time, so the artifact stays self-contained with no runtime highlighter dependency.
Local edits round-trip as a `dossier.edits/v1` packet keyed by block id, with `text`, `lang`, and `targetPath`.

items.q: Where do glossary terms get used?
items.a: A `glossary` block defines terms that resolve `[[Term]]` tooltips anywhere else in the document.

items.label: Shiki syntax highlighter
items.signal: Authoritative source for supported languages and themes
items.use: Pick `lang` values that Shiki recognizes for `code` blocks

items.label: Zod validation
items.signal: Boundary validation patterns used in the TypeScript sample

terms.definition: The embedded source-of-truth JSON island that every block is rendered from.
A `dossier.edits/v1` export produced by `code-editor` blocks, keyed by block id.
The required `type` field on every block that selects its renderer and schema.
A local image or font inlined as a data URI so the artifact stays fully self-contained.

blocks.title: Code review
blocks.subtitle: Proposed patches and unified-diff review surfaces.

blocks.heading: Patch review surfaces
blocks.markdown: Two block families drive code review. The **patch-set** records proposed edit packets with `operation`, `status`, and `risk`, plus files, work items, verification, and an optional unified diff. The **diff-view** parses a unified diff into a file-first reviewable surface. Below each enum value appears at least once so every chip and badge renders.

patches.title: Add token store module
patches.summary: Introduce a narrow `tokenStore` helper so persistence is no longer read inline.

patches.title: Route session reads through the store
patches.summary: Replace the inline `localStorage` read in `readSession` with `tokenStore.read()`.

patches.title: Delete legacy cookie shim
patches.summary: Remove the unused `cookie-shim.ts` fallback now that every caller uses the store.

patches.title: Rename session helper file
patches.summary: Rename `session.ts` to `session-reader.ts` to match the single-responsibility naming.

patches.title: Add refresh flow and prune old guard
patches.summary: Mixed packet: adds `refresh.ts`, edits `session.ts`, and drops a dead guard branch.

blocks.title: Full multi-file diff
blocks.summary: Standalone unified diff for file-first review: an added file, a deleted file, and a modified hunk.

blocks.heading: Reading the figures
blocks.markdown: Every visual in this family is **self-contained**: figures inline as data URIs, charts and diagrams render to SVG at build time, and math compiles to MathML with no external fonts. The throughput numbers below are sampled from a single release window [^g5-sampling], so treat the absolute values as illustrative rather than a benchmark [^g5-method].

blocks.alt: Two rounded nodes labeled Author and Render connected by a build arrow
blocks.caption: Figure 1. The author-to-render path, drawn as an inline SVG so the artifact stays fully self-contained.

blocks.title: Render pipeline (DOT)
blocks.spec: digraph pipeline { rankdir=LR; node [shape=box, style=rounded]; json [label="dossier.json"]; validate [label="validate"]; render [label="render"]; html [label="static html"]; json -> validate -> render -> html; validate -> json [label="errors", style=dashed]; }

items.text: Sampled across the `0.5.x` and `0.6.x` release windows. Values are medians, not peaks.
Build timings measured on a warm cache with **Shiki** highlighting enabled. See the [methodology notes](https://example.com/method).
All durations are wall-clock milliseconds unless otherwise stated.

blocks.title: Decisions
blocks.subtitle: Matrices, risks, assumptions, actions, triage, gates, decision log.

risks.risk: Owner field left blank on import
risks.mitigation: Default to the triage rotation lead.

risks.risk: Schema drift between board and export
risks.mitigation: Pin the export schema and add a contract test.
Publish a one-page rubric with worked examples.

risks.risk: Scope creep during the freeze window
risks.mitigation: Freeze the matrix criteria first, then queue new options for the next cycle.

risks.risk: Export packet grows past the inline budget
risks.mitigation: Lazy-load reference `blocks` only when a row expands.

items.statement: Reviewers will tick decisions in a single sitting per cycle.
The exported decisions JSON round-trips back into the board without loss.
A shared severity rubric removes most disagreement.
Should rejected candidates stay searchable or move to an archive view?
Do we need per-candidate audit history, or is the latest verdict enough?
Can we drop the legacy `priority` import field entirely?

items.title: Publish the severity rubric to the team wiki
Wire the decisions export into the nightly sync
Backfill owners on the 12 unassigned rows
Add a contract test for the export schema

candidates.title: Saved filter views
candidates.summary: Let reviewers pin a named filter and reopen it later.
candidates.body:
Reviewers repeatedly rebuild the same `status:open severity:high` filter. A **saved view** stores the active query, sort, and column set under a name.

Views are local-first and sync opportunistically, so an offline reviewer never loses their pinned queries.

candidates.title: Bulk verdict apply
candidates.summary: Apply one verdict to every selected row at once.
candidates.body: Selecting a range and choosing **Approve** writes the same verdict and an optional shared note to each row, then logs them individually in the export packet so the audit trail stays per-candidate.

blocks.title: Audit note.
blocks.body: Each row still exports independently so blame stays precise.

candidates.title: Inline severity rubric
candidates.summary: Show the scoring rubric next to the severity control.
candidates.body: Disagreement on severity comes from reviewers guessing the scale. A hover card surfaces the **rubric** with one worked example per level so scoring stays consistent.

candidates.title: Archive rejected rows
candidates.summary: Move rejected candidates out of the active queue.
candidates.body:
Rejected rows clutter the default view. Archiving hides them from the main queue while keeping them searchable under an **Archived** tab.

Deferred until the saved-views work lands, since archive is just a system-managed view.

blocks.title: Approve the triage cycle close-out?
blocks.prompt: Approve, revise, skip, defer, split, retry, or block closing this triage cycle and exporting the decisions packet.

decisions.decision: Host the triage queue on managed Postgres
decisions.rationale: Broad cross-team access outweighed the slightly higher setup cost; embedded SQLite could not serve external reviewers.

decisions.decision: Defer the rejected-row archive to the next cycle
decisions.rationale: Archive is a thin layer over saved views, so building it first would duplicate work. Sequence it after views ship.

blocks.title: Process & trust
blocks.subtitle: Implementation, verification, evidence, findings, integration, release, trust.

items.title: Extract token store
items.summary: Move token persistence behind a narrow `tokenStore` helper so callers stop touching `localStorage` directly.
risks: Stale tokens if migration runs mid-session.
items.body: The current session module reads and writes `localStorage` in **eight** places. Consolidating into one helper keeps the public API stable and makes the storage layer swappable for SSR.

items.title: Add CSRF guard to mutation routes
items.summary: Mutation endpoints accept cross-origin posts. Add a double-submit token check at the boundary.
risks: Token rotation could break long-lived tabs.
items.body: Revise needed: the proposed guard rejects same-site `fetch` calls that omit the header. Scope the check to `POST | PUT | DELETE` and exempt the health probe.

items.title: Migrate legacy cache adapter
items.summary: The v1 cache adapter blocks the upgrade. Migration is risky and underspecified.
risks: No rollback path once keys are rewritten.
items.body: Blocked pending a reversible migration plan. Do **not** proceed until the key-rewrite step is idempotent and benchmarked under load.

items.title: Stabilize flaky upload test
items.summary: `upload.spec` fails ~1 in 12 runs on CI due to an unawaited promise.
items.body: Retry: first fix reduced but did not eliminate the flake. Await the stream close before asserting and re-run 20x to confirm.

blocks.title: Verification
blocks.summary: Commands run during this packet, with expected and actual output captured.

runs.title: Type check
runs.notes: Add a null guard before returning the session.

runs.title: End-to-end smoke
runs.expected: Login flow completes in a real browser.

runs.title: Performance benchmark
runs.actual: Skipped: cache migration is blocked, benchmark not applicable yet.

items.title: Auth unit test log
items.body: `npm test -- auth` output: **23 passing**. Captured from the local run before commit.

items.title: CSRF rejection trace
items.body: Staging access log showing same-site `fetch` calls rejected with 403 after the first guard draft.

items.title: OWASP CSRF cheat sheet
items.body: Reference for the double-submit cookie pattern used in the guard design.

findings.title: Verbose debug logging left in handler
findings.body: The upload handler logs the full request body on every call, which is noisy and may leak filenames.
findings.recommendation: Gate the log behind `DEBUG` or drop it.

findings.title: Missing null guard on session read
findings.body: `tokenStore.read()` can return null but `readSession()` dereferences it without a check.
findings.recommendation: Return a typed `null` session instead of throwing.

findings.title: CSRF guard skips PUT and DELETE
findings.body: Only `POST` is checked. State-changing `PUT` and `DELETE` routes are unprotected.
findings.recommendation: Apply the guard to all unsafe methods.

findings.title: Auth token written to plaintext log
findings.body: On error, the session middleware logs the bearer token verbatim, exposing it to anyone with log access.
findings.recommendation: Redact the token before logging. Rotate any tokens already captured.

threads.subject: Keep the public session API stable

comments.author: Kyle
comments.body: Before we refactor, confirm `readSession()` keeps the same return shape. Downstream relies on it.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Confirmed. The helper is internal only and `readSession()` signature is unchanged. Added a test that asserts the shape.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Proposing a `redact()` util that masks all but the last 4 chars. Should we redact in the logger or at the call site?

comments.author: Kyle
comments.body: Logger-level, so we cannot forget it at a call site. Make it the default for any field named `token` or `authorization`.

cycles.title: Producer to consumer
cycles.summary: Built the package locally and ran the consumer suite against it. **All green**, no API drift.
cycles.body: Linked `dossier` into `lumen` via `npm link` and ran the consumer integration suite. No breaking changes surfaced.

cycles.title: Consumer feedback loop
cycles.summary: Consumer requested a smaller export surface. Trimming the public types now.
cycles.body: Awaiting confirmation that the trimmed `index.d.ts` still covers the consumer's call sites.

blocks.title: Integration report
blocks.summary: Closeout for the dossier to lumen handoff this cycle.
blocks.nextStep: Publish 0.5.2 and pin lumen to the released range.

items.title: API contract
items.summary: Public block types unchanged. No breaking change for the consumer.

items.title: Type exports
items.summary: Trimmed export surface; consumer call sites still resolve.

items.title: Runtime packet shape
items.summary: Process packet gained a field; consumer reader updated but not yet released.

blocks.title: Upstream response
blocks.request: Accept a compatibility patch so the bounded editor can target `data-code-editor` without a custom build.
blocks.response: Issue opened. Awaiting maintainer triage; a minimal repro and the proposed patch are attached.
blocks.nextStep: Ping the thread in one week if there is no response.

gates.title: Type check clean
gates.evidence: 1 error in session.ts; must fix before tagging

blocks.title: Trust report
blocks.summary: Each claim links to source ids and evidence ids so a reviewer can audit the basis for it.

sources.label: npm test
sources.summary: Local verification command run before commit.

sources.label: Staging access log
sources.summary: Captured from the shared staging environment.

sources.label: OWASP cheat sheet
sources.summary: External reference for the CSRF design.

sources.label: Vendor SLA page
sources.summary: Marketing page; uptime figure not independently confirmed.

claims.claim: The auth test suite passes on the release branch.
claims.notes: Reproduced locally twice with identical output.

claims.claim: The CSRF guard now covers all unsafe methods.
claims.notes: POST is covered; PUT and DELETE coverage is implemented but not yet retested in staging.

claims.claim: No auth tokens are written to logs.
claims.notes: Redaction is proposed but not yet merged, so this is not confirmed.

claims.claim: The vendor guarantees 99.99% uptime for the cache layer.
claims.notes: Our own staging logs show two outages this month, contradicting the marketing figure.
Rejected: the key-rewrite step is not idempotent and dropped reads during the dry run.

blocks.title: Closeout receipt
blocks.summary: What this packet produced and what remains before release.
risks: Token redaction is not yet merged.
followUps: Fix the typecheck error before tagging.
Retest CSRF on PUT and DELETE in staging.

blocks.notes: Verification ran locally. The typecheck failure and unmerged token redaction are the main open risks; the uptime claim is unconfirmed and treated as disputed.
